Consumer Report : Any communication of information by a consumer reporting agency related to a consumer 's creditworthiness, credit standing, or credit capacity that is used or expected to be used for credit, employment, or insurance purposes.

Consumer Reporting Agency ( CRA ) : Any person or entity that regularly assembles or evaluates consumer credit information for the purpose of providing reports to third parties.

File : All information on a consumer that is recorded and retained by a CRA. 

Person : Includes individuals, partnerships, corporations, trusts, and others.

consumer_narrative:
To Whom It May Concern, I am contacting you to formally notify you that I am a victim of identity theft. Fraudulent accounts and/or transactions have been made using my personal information without my knowledge or consent. I did not authorize these actions and they do not reflect my financial activity or responsibility.

Pursuant to 15 U.S. Code 1681c2, also known as Section 605B of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), I am exercising my legal right to request that all information resulting from this identity theft be blocked from my credit file. This section mandates that a consumer reporting agency must block any information resulting from identity theft within four ( 4 ) business days of receiving : 1. Proof of the consumers identity, 2. A copy of an Identity Theft Report ( such as one filed with the FTC or a police report ), 3. The identification of the fraudulent information, and 4. A statement from the consumer that the information is not related to any transaction they made.

According to www.justice.gov, Identity theft and identity fraud are terms used to refer to all types of crime in which someone wrongfully obtains and uses another person 's personal data in some way that involves fraud or deception, typically for economic gain. The Department of Justice prosecutes cases of identity theft and fraud under a variety of federal statutes. In the fall of 1998, for example, Congress passed the Identity Theft and Assumption Deterrence Act. This legislation created a new offense of identity theft, which prohibits " knowingly transfer [ ring ] or us [ ing ], without lawful authority, a means of identification of another person with the intent to commit, or to aid or abet, any unlawful activity that constitutes a violation of Federal law, or that constitutes a felony under any applicable State or local law. ( a ) Block. Except as otherwise provided in this section, a consumer reporting agency shall block the reporting of any information in the file of a consumer that the consumer identifies as information that resulted from an alleged identity theft, not later than 4 business days after the date of receipt by such agency of ( 1 ) appropriate proof of the identity of the consumer ; ( 2 ) a copy of an identity theft report ; ( 3 ) the identification of such information by the consumer; and ( 4 ) a statement by the consumer that the information is not information relating to any transaction by the consumer. ( b ) Notification. A consumer reporting agency shall promptly notify the furnisher of information identified by the consumer under subsection ( a ) of this section ( 1 ) that the information may be a result of identity theft ; ( 2 ) that an identity theft report has been filed ; ( 3 ) that a block has been requested under this section; and ( 4 ) of the effective dates of the block. To Whom it May Concern, I am submitting this formal complaint pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), 15 U.S.C 1681 et seq., regarding the continued reporting of TRANSWORLD SYSTEMS account that is CLOSED yet still appearing on my consumer credit reports. TRANSWORLD SYSTEMS is continuing to furnish information on this closed account in a manner that is inaccurate, misleading, incomplete, and not reflective of the accounts current legal status, in violation of federal law. Under FCRA 623 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ), a furnisher may not report information it knows or has reasonable cause to believe is inaccurate. Additionally, FCRA 623 ( a ) ( 2 ) requires furnishers to promptly correct or update information so that it is complete and accurate. Further, reporting a closed account in a manner that adversely impacts my creditworthiness without proper qualification or clarity constitutes materially misleading reporting, which courts have repeatedly held to be actionable under the FCRA. product: Payday loan, title loan, personal loan, or advance loan
consumer_narrative:
In or aroundXXXX XXXX, I obtained an online installment loan from Bright Lending while residing in Illinois. 
The loan carried an annual percentage rate of approximately599 % or higher, far exceeding the36 % APR cap under the Illinois Predatory Loan Prevention Act ( PLPA ). Bright Lending is not licensed by the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation to issue consumer loans in Illinois.

Despite providing clear evidence that these transactions were conscious, voluntary, and legitimate ( including screenshots, contracts, communication records, receipts, and proof of services delivered ), PayPal failed to defend against the chargebacks.

Additionally, the financial institutions involved failed to comply with Regulation XXXX, which requires them to perform reasonable investigations before approving a chargeback.
